folio.label: 90v
folio.l: Now Thomas help , for Seynt charitee
And doun a noon , he sette hi~ on his knee
¢ This syke man , wex my wood , for Ire
He wolde that the frere , had ben a fire
Witú his fals , dissimulacione
Swicú thyngö as is , in myn possessione
Q¶ he , that may I ©eue ©ow & noon other
ñe seye me thus , how that I am ©oure brother
¢ ña certes q¶ the frere , trostetú wel
I toke oure dame , the &lbar;re of oure seal
¢ Now wel q¶ he , & sumwhat shal I ©eue
Vn to ©oure holy Couent , whil I lyue
And in thyn hand , æè shalt it han a noone
On this condicione , and other noone
That æè departe it , so , my dere brother
That euäy frere , haue as meche as other
Thus shalt æè swere , on thy ¸fessione
Witú outen fraude , or cauellacione
¢ I swere it q¶ the frere , vp on my feitú
And ther witú al , his hond in his he leitú
Loo heere my feitú , in me shal be no lak
¢ Thanne put thyn hand , a doun rigút be my bak
Seide this man , and grope wel behynde
By nethe my buttok , there shalt æè fynde
A thyngö that I haue , hid , in preuytee
¢ A thougút this frere , that shal go witú me
And doun his hand , he launched to the clift
In hope , for to fynde there , a ©ift
¢ And whan this syke man , felt this frere
A boute his towel , gropen heere and there
A mydde his hand , he leet the frere a fart
There is no capel , drawyngö in a cart
That mygút han leete a fart , of swicú a soun~
¢ The frere vp stirt , as dotú a wood lyouö
A fals cherl q¶ he , for goddis bones
This hast æè for despyt , don for the nones
Thow shalt a bye , this fart if I may
His meyne / whicú , æ herden swicú a fray
Comen lepyngö In , and chased out the frere
And fortú he gotú , witú a ful angry chere
And fette his felawe , there as lay his stoor
He loked as he were , a wylde Boor
And grynt witú the tetú , so was he wrotú
A sturdy paas , doun to the courte he gotú
Where as there woned , a man of gret hounour
To whom that he was , alwey confessour
